sections.paragraph:
f. a city, a town, i.q. עִיר, but used almost exclusively in poetry, Isa 1:21, 26 22:2 25:2 26:5 32:13 Psa 48:3 Pro 10:15 Job 39:7 see, however, Deu 2:36 1Ki 1:41, 45 1Ki 1:45. (Syriac ܩܪܐܶ id., Arabic قَرْيَةُ, قُرْيَةُ a city, also a village). Root קָרָה Piel, to frame, to build. Compare קֶרֶת.

Proper names of towns are:-(a) קִרְיַת אַרְבַּע [Kirjath-arba], Gen 23:2 Jos 15:54 20:7 with art. קִרְיַת הָאַרְבַּע Neh 11:25 (i.e. city of Arba, one of the Anakim, see אַרְבַּע , not Quadricomium, four towns), the ancient name of Hebron, but still used in the time of Nehemiah (Neh. loc. cit.).-(b) קִרְיַת בַּעַל [Kirjathbaal], the town which is more frequently called קִרְיַת יְעָרִים (see letter d), Jos 15:60 18:14 and בַּעֲלָה No. 2, a.-(c) קִרְיַת־חֻצֹות (town of villages), [Kirjathhuzoth], a town of the Moabites, Num 22:39.-(d) קִרְיַת־יְעָרִים (city of the woods), [Kirjath-jearim], on the confines of the tribe of Judah and Benjamin, Jos 9:17 18:15 Jud. 18:12 1Sa 6:21 with art. קִרְיַת הַיְּעָרִים, Jer 26:20 contractedly קִרְיַת־עָרִים Ezr 2:25 and even קִרְיַת, Jos 18:28 elsewhere also קִרְיַת־בַּעַל, see letter b.-(e) קִרְיַת־סַנָּה, Jos 15:49 (city of palm-trees, compare סַנְסִנִּים), [Kirjath-sannah], and קִרְיַת־סֵפֶר (city of books), [Kirjath-sepher], in the tribe of Judah, elsewhere דְּבִיר, Jos 15:15, 16 Jos 15:16 Jud 1:11, 12 Jdg 1:12.-(f) קִרְיַת עָרִים, [Kirjath-arim], see letter d.-(g) קִרְיָתַיִם (double city), [Kiriathaim]-(a) in the tribe of Reuben, Num 32:37 Jos 13:19 afterwards subject to Moab, Jer 48:1, 23 Jer 48:23 Eze 25:9.-(β) in the tribe of Naphtali, 1Ch 6:6 elsewhere קַרְתָּן.

קריה

and קִרְיָא Chald. id. Ezr 4:10, seqq.
